title: Job Vacancies at Ascent Resources
content:
Ascent Resources, an HR Consulting Firm is recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the following positions below:

1.) Front Desk Officer (Female)

Job Location: Anthony Village, Lagos Mainland - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	3–5 years of experience in a front desk, receptionist, or customer-facing role
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Professional appearance and positive attitude
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and basic administrative tasks
	Ability to manage calls, visitors, and general office coordination efficiently


General Requirements


	Must be reliable, disciplined, and able to work with minimal supervision
	Candidates should reside within or around Lagos Mainland.


Application Closing Date: 6th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

2.) Managing Director (Micro Finance Bank)

Job Location: Akute, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time
Organization: Newly Licensed Microfinance Bank
Reporting To: Board of Directors

The Mandate


	Our client seeks an exceptional Managing Director to lead its establishment and scale it into a high-performing, digitally driven institution.
	This role requires a commercially astute and visionary leader with the capacity to define strategy, drive growth, ensure regulatory excellence, and build a resilient organization from the ground up.


Core Accountabilities


	Strategy & Growth: Define and execute the bank’s strategic agenda, drive market penetration, and lead partnerships and expansion initiatives.
	Financial Performance: Deliver strong revenue growth through deposits, lending, and product innovation while ensuring sustainable profitability.
	Governance & Compliance: Ensure full regulatory compliance and embed robust risk management and internal control frameworks.
	Operational & Digital Leadership: Establish efficient operations and lead digital transformation through fintech integration and customer-centric innovation.


Performance Priorities


	Growth in deposits, lending, and customer base
	Sustained profitability and cost efficiency
	Full regulatory compliance
	Enhanced operational efficiency and service delivery
	Successful digital and fintech adoption


Candidate Profile


	Degree in Finance, Banking, Business Administration, or related discipline; MBA or equivalent preferred.
	Minimum 12 years of experience in financial services, with at least 5 years in executive leadership.
	Strong expertise in microfinance banking, fintech, and regulatory frameworks.
	Demonstrated success in driving growth, profitability, and institutional performance.
	Strong stakeholder engagement skills and established industry network.
	Strategic, disciplined, and execution-focused leadership style.


Application Closing Date: 6th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Preference will be given to a qualified female candidate of Eastern Nigerian (Igbo) heritage.

 

 

3.) Chief Finance Officer (CFO)

Job Location: Anthony Village, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 


	This is not a traditional finance role.
	It is a high-impact position for a hands-on strategist ready to shape the financial future of a multi-industry powerhouse.


Key Responsibilities


	Craft and execute a Group-wide financial strategy that aligns with ambitious growth plans across diverse sectors.
	Lead budgeting, forecasting, treasury, cash flow optimisation, and capital raising (equity, debt, and infrastructure finance).
	Drive profitability, revenue assurance, cost optimisation, and robust internal controls while strengthening risk management and compliance.
	Deliver strategic insights on investments, pricing, partnerships, and expansion to the CEO and Board.
	Build and inspire a high-performing finance team anchored on excellence, transparency, and accountability.


Requirements


	Fully qualified Chartered Accountant (ACA, ACCA, or equivalent).
	An MBA or CFA is a strong advantage.
	15–18 years of progressive experience, withat least 7 yearsat the CFO, Finance Director, or Head of Finance level.
	Proven track record acrossthree or moreof our sectors: Oil & Gas, Media, Technology, Power, Construction, or Financial Services.
	Demonstrated success in capital raising, IFRS reporting, cash flow optimisation, and enterprise risk management.
	Strategic, commercially sharp, and resilient — with the ability to thrive in a fast-paced Lagos conglomerate environment.


Application Closing Date: 5th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at Olam Nigeria Limited
content:
Olam Nigeria Limited is located in Lagos, Nigeria and is part of the Farm Support Services Industry. Olam Nigeria Limited has 3,000 employees at this location and generates $222.01 million in sales (USD).

With offices and operational units across all geopolitical zones of the Nigerian Federation, Olam has a wide and growing network of farmers, suppliers, wholesalers, local buying agents (LBAs), customers and service providers. Today, these networks encompass approximately 500,000 farmers and have created tens of thousands of jobs in indirect employment.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Operations Officer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Operations Officer is responsible for overseeing the day-to-day operational activities of the organization to ensure efficiency, productivity, and compliance with company standards.
	The role involves coordinating processes, managing resources, and supporting the implementation of business strategies to achieve organizational goals.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate and monitor daily operational activities across departments to ensure smooth workflow.
	Develop and implement operational policies, procedures, and best practices.
	Manage resources effectively, including personnel, materials, and equipment.
	Track performance metrics and prepare regular operational reports for management.
	Identify operational challenges and implement solutions to improve efficiency and productivity.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, industry regulations, and safety standards.
	Liaise with internal teams, vendors, and external stakeholders to support business operations.
	Oversee inventory management, logistics, and supply chain processes where applicable.
	Assist in budgeting, cost control, and financial planning related to operations.
	Support project execution and ensure timely delivery of operational objectives.
	Drive continuous improvement initiatives to optimize processes and reduce costs.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Operations Management, or a related field.
	2–5 years of experience in operations or administrative roles.
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Excellent problem-solving and decision-making skills.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.


Key Competencies:


	Leadership and teamwork
	Time management
	Attention to detail
	Analytical thinking
	Process improvement
	Adaptability.


Salary
N170,000 - N220,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date: 29th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

2.) Assistant Secretary

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Assistant Secretary provides administrative and organizational support to senior management, ensuring efficient coordination of office operations.
	The role involves managing documentation, scheduling meetings, maintaining records, and supporting communication across departments within the organization’s food production, agricultural, and distribution operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide day-to-day administrative support to the Company Secretary and management team
	Prepare, organize, and maintain official documents, reports, and records
	Schedule meetings, appointments, and coordinate logistics for internal and external engagements
	Record and distribute minutes of meetings accurately and promptly
	Handle incoming and outgoing correspondence (emails, letters, calls) professionally
	Maintain confidentiality of sensitive corporate information
	Assist in compliance documentation and regulatory filings where required
	Support communication between departments, including production, distribution, and supply chain units
	Manage office supplies and ensure smooth administrative operations
	Assist in coordinating company programs such as farmer support initiatives and internal projects.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Secretarial Studies, or related fields
	Proven experience in administrative or secretarial roles (preferably in FMCG, agriculture, or manufacturing sectors)
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
	High level of discretion and professionalism
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.


Key Skills:


	Communication and interpersonal skills
	Problem-solving ability
	Confidentiality and integrity
	Time management and attention to detail
	Record keeping and documentation.


Salary
N120,000 - N160,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date
20th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.)  Quality Assurance (QA) Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Quality Assurance (QA) Manager is responsiblefor ensuring that all food products produced meet the highest standards of safety, quality, and regulatory compliance.
	This role is critical in maintaining consumer trust, especially for fortified and affordable food products targeted at local markets.


Key Responsibilities
Quality Control Systems:


	Develop, implement, and maintain quality assurance policies and procedures across all production lines (flour, rice, feed, etc.).
	Food Safety Compliance:
	Ensure compliance with local and international food safety standards (e.g., NAFDAC, HACCP, ISO standards).
	Product Testing & Inspection:
	Oversee laboratory testing of raw materials and finished products to ensure they meet nutritional and safety specifications.
	Process Monitoring:
	Monitor production processes to identify and correct deviations that may affect product quality.
	Supplier Quality Management:
	Evaluate and approve suppliers of raw materials (e.g., wheat, rice paddy, feed ingredients) to ensure consistent quality.
	Documentation & Reporting:
	Maintain accurate records of inspections, audits, and quality reports for regulatory and internal use.
	Training & Awareness:
	Train production staff on hygiene, food safety, and quality standards to ensure consistent compliance.
	Audit Management:
	Coordinate internal and external audits and ensure corrective actions are implemented where necessary.


Educational Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Food Science, Microbiology, Biochemistry, or related field
	Professional certifications in food safety (e.g., HACCP, ISO 22000) are an advantage.


Key Skills & Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of food safety standards and regulations
	Attention to detail and analytical thinking
	Problem-solving and decision-making skills
	Leadership and team management
	Good communication and reporting skills.


Salary
N160,000 - N200,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date: 11th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "HR & Admin Manager",
  "description": "Refine Concepts - Our client in the Agro commodity business, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR &amp; Admin Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;

Role&nbsp;Summary


	Our client in the Agro commodity business is looking for a hands-on HR &amp; Admin Manager who will primarily drive recruitment, employee management, and HR processes, while also overseeing essential administrative and operational support functions.
	This is a ground-level execution role, not a purely strategic HR position. The candidate should be actively involved in hiring, managing people, resolving employee issues, and ensuring discipline and structure, along with supporting admin operations like warehouses, drivers, and expat arrangements.


Key&nbsp;Responsibilities
Recruitment &amp; Talent Management (Core Focus):


	Lead end-to-end recruitment for blue-collar and junior to mid-level roles
	Source candidates through local networks, agencies, and referrals
	Conduct initial screenings and coordinate interviews
	Ensure timely hiring to support business operations
	Maintain a pipeline of candidates for critical roles.


HR Operations &amp; Employee Management (Core Focus):


	Manage attendance, leave records, and employee documentation
	Ensure proper onboarding and induction of new employees
	Handle employee grievances, disciplinary actions, and conflict resolution
	Implement HR policies and ensure compliance with local labor laws
	Drive employee retention and engagement initiatives
	Maintain HR records and reporting.


Performance &amp; Workforce Discipline:


	Monitor workforce productivity and discipline (especially drivers, warehouse staff)
	Support managers in handling underperformance and absenteeism
	Ensure proper structuring of shifts, duties, and workforce allocation.


Government &amp; Compliance (HR-related):


	Handle statutory compliance related to employees
	Liaise with government bodies for labor-related matters
	Ensure all employee records and documentation are compliant.


Administration &amp; Operations (Supporting Role):


	Warehouse &amp; Facility Administration
	Oversee basic administration of warehouses and offices
	Ensure cleanliness, maintenance, and utilities are functioning.


Fleet &amp; Driver Coordination:


	Monitor driver discipline, attendance, and basic compliance
	Ensure vehicle documentation is up to date
	Expats Accommodation Support
	Assist in setting up accommodation for expats
	Ensure basic facilities, utilities, and coordination.


Vendor Management:


	Coordinate with admin vendors and service providers
	Support cost control and basic negotiations.


Execution &amp; Problem Solving:


	Act as the first point of contact for people-related issues
	Resolve day-to-day HR and admin challenges proactively
	Ensure smooth functioning of workforce and operations with minimal escalation.


Candidate&nbsp;Profile
Experience:


	7 - 10 years in HR, recruitment, or people operations
	Experience in manufacturing, warehouse, or field-based environments preferred.


Personality:


	Hands-on and execution-focused
	Strong ownership and accountability
	Practical, street-smart, and solution-oriented
	Comfortable dealing with blue-collar workforce.


Skills:


	Strong recruitment and people management skills
	Knowledge of Nigerian labor laws and HR practices
	Good coordination and negotiation abilities
	Basic administrative and operational understanding.


Salary
N650,000 - N900,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should their application to:&nbsp;refineconceptjobs@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

HR & Admin Manager Job at Refine Concepts




        
    

        
        Refine Concepts - Our client in the Agro commodity business, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR & Admin Manager

Job Location: Lagos 

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Our client in the Agro commodity business is looking for a hands-on HR & Admin Manager who will primarily drive recruitment, employee management, and HR processes, while also overseeing essential administrative and operational support functions.
	This is a ground-level execution role, not a purely strategic HR position. The candidate should be actively involved in hiring, managing people, resolving employee issues, and ensuring discipline and structure, along with supporting admin operations like warehouses, drivers, and expat arrangements.


Key Responsibilities
Recruitment & Talent Management (Core Focus):


	Lead end-to-end recruitment for blue-collar and junior to mid-level roles
	Source candidates through local networks, agencies, and referrals
	Conduct initial screenings and coordinate interviews
	Ensure timely hiring to support business operations
	Maintain a pipeline of candidates for critical roles.


HR Operations & Employee Management (Core Focus):


	Manage attendance, leave records, and employee documentation
	Ensure proper onboarding and induction of new employees
	Handle employee grievances, disciplinary actions, and conflict resolution
	Implement HR policies and ensure compliance with local labor laws
	Drive employee retention and engagement initiatives
	Maintain HR records and reporting.


Performance & Workforce Discipline:


	Monitor workforce productivity and discipline (especially drivers, warehouse staff)
	Support managers in handling underperformance and absenteeism
	Ensure proper structuring of shifts, duties, and workforce allocation.


Government & Compliance (HR-related):


	Handle statutory compliance related to employees
	Liaise with government bodies for labor-related matters
	Ensure all employee records and documentation are compliant.


Administration & Operations (Supporting Role):


	Warehouse & Facility Administration
	Oversee basic administration of warehouses and offices
	Ensure cleanliness, maintenance, and utilities are functioning.


Fleet & Driver Coordination:


	Monitor driver discipline, attendance, and basic compliance
	Ensure vehicle documentation is up to date
	Expats Accommodation Support
	Assist in setting up accommodation for expats
	Ensure basic facilities, utilities, and coordination.


Vendor Management:


	Coordinate with admin vendors and service providers
	Support cost control and basic negotiations.


Execution & Problem Solving:


	Act as the first point of contact for people-related issues
	Resolve day-to-day HR and admin challenges proactively
	Ensure smooth functioning of workforce and operations with minimal escalation.


Candidate Profile
Experience:


	7 - 10 years in HR, recruitment, or people operations
	Experience in manufacturing, warehouse, or field-based environments preferred.


Personality:


	Hands-on and execution-focused
	Strong ownership and accountability
	Practical, street-smart, and solution-oriented
	Comfortable dealing with blue-collar workforce.


Skills:


	Strong recruitment and people management skills
	Knowledge of Nigerian labor laws and HR practices
	Good coordination and negotiation abilities
	Basic administrative and operational understanding.


Salary
N650,000 - N900,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should their application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Operations Officer Job at Olam Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Olam Nigeria Limited - We manufacture and distribute packaged food products to African markets, addressing localised nutrition needs by offering affordable and fortified products. We offer good quality, inexpensive feed, and day-old-chicks to farmers, which also contributes to foods security. Our mill farm produces 36,000 metric tonnes of rice for local markets under brands such as Mama's Pride and Mama's Choice.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Operations Officer is responsible for overseeing the day-to-day operational activities of the organization to ensure efficiency, productivity, and compliance with company standards.
	The role involves coordinating processes, managing resources, and supporting the implementation of business strategies to achieve organizational goals.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate and monitor daily operational activities across departments to ensure smooth workflow.
	Develop and implement operational policies, procedures, and best practices.
	Manage resources effectively, including personnel, materials, and equipment.
	Track performance metrics and prepare regular operational reports for management.
	Identify operational challenges and implement solutions to improve efficiency and productivity.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, industry regulations, and safety standards.
	Liaise with internal teams, vendors, and external stakeholders to support business operations.
	Oversee inventory management, logistics, and supply chain processes where applicable.
	Assist in budgeting, cost control, and financial planning related to operations.
	Support project execution and ensure timely delivery of operational objectives.
	Drive continuous improvement initiatives to optimize processes and reduce costs.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Operations Management, or a related field.
	2–5 years of experience in operations or administrative roles.
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Excellent problem-solving and decision-making skills.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.


Key Competencies:


	Leadership and teamwork
	Time management
	Attention to detail
	Analytical thinking
	Process improvement
	Adaptability.


Salary
N170,000 - N220,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "UI/UX Designer",
  "description": "Gate84 is a technology company building the digital infrastructure for a functional society. We aim to bridge the gap between real-world problems and technical solutions by creating a platform where verified experts can propose practical, peer-reviewed solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: UI/UX Designer

Job Location:&nbsp;Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Own the complete design process from research, initial wireframes to high-fidelity prototypes.
	Build, scale, and maintain a comprehensive design system in Figma to ensure visual consistency.
	Collaborate with product, development and leadership to balance user needs, business needs and technical constraints.
	Design and prototype responsive mobile/web interfaces, administrative dashboards and landing pages.
	Plan and execute user research and conduct usability testing to validate design ideas/assumptions.
	Map out clear and engaging user journeys for multiple distinct user personas.
	Create interactive, high-fidelity prototypes to effectively communicate interaction design, micro-animations, and state changes.
	Execute seamless design-to-development handoff with the Mobile/Frontend teams.


Requirements


	3+ years of experience
	A portfolio showcasing user experience, visual design, and UI work
	Attention to detail and creative thinking.


Benefits and opportunities


	Salary: N250,000 - N350,000 monthly.
	Monthly transport allowance
	HMO Coverage
	Gym membership
	Opportunity to grow and lead a team.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and links of their LinkedIn/portfolio/projects to:&nbsp;joel@gate84.io&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  },

UI/UX Designer Job at Gate84




        
    

        
        Gate84 is a technology company building the digital infrastructure for a functional society. We aim to bridge the gap between real-world problems and technical solutions by creating a platform where verified experts can propose practical, peer-reviewed solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: UI/UX Designer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Own the complete design process from research, initial wireframes to high-fidelity prototypes.
	Build, scale, and maintain a comprehensive design system in Figma to ensure visual consistency.
	Collaborate with product, development and leadership to balance user needs, business needs and technical constraints.
	Design and prototype responsive mobile/web interfaces, administrative dashboards and landing pages.
	Plan and execute user research and conduct usability testing to validate design ideas/assumptions.
	Map out clear and engaging user journeys for multiple distinct user personas.
	Create interactive, high-fidelity prototypes to effectively communicate interaction design, micro-animations, and state changes.
	Execute seamless design-to-development handoff with the Mobile/Frontend teams.


Requirements


	3+ years of experience
	A portfolio showcasing user experience, visual design, and UI work
	Attention to detail and creative thinking.


Benefits and opportunities


	Salary: N250,000 - N350,000 monthly.
	Monthly transport allowance
	HMO Coverage
	Gym membership
	Opportunity to grow and lead a team.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and links of their LinkedIn/portfolio/projects to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
